title: Trinity Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
## May 27, 2018 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon was preached on Trinity Sunday, the first Sunday after Pentecost in the Christian calendar, marking the conclusion of the Easter season and transition into Ordinary Time. Under the Revised Common Lectionary (used by most Episcopal parishes), the readings would have included Proverbs 8:1-4, 22-31 (Wisdom's role in creation), Psalm 8 (humanity's place in God's creation), Romans 5:1-5 (peace through Christ and the Holy Spirit), and John 16:12-15 (the Spirit's role in revealing truth). Trinity Sunday is one of the few Sundays centered on doctrine rather than a historical event or biblical narrative, making it traditionally a theological and contemplative observance.

---

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of its General Convention (held in Austin, Texas in July 2018 was upcoming, so the church was in pre-convention season with debate intensifying over sexuality and inclusion resolutions). In May 2018, the church was navigating tensions around LGBTQ+ inclusion and marriage, with various dioceses preparing resolutions for the summer convention. The Diocese of Colorado (which includes Saint John's Cathedral) was a relatively progressive voice within the national church during this period, though specific diocesan news for this exact week is not readily available in public records.

---

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical late spring weather with temperatures warming into the 70s-80s Fahrenheit range. The city was focused on local elections and governance issues; Denver Mayor Michael Hancock was in his first year as mayor (elected 2019 runoff pending), and the city was discussing development and housing affordability. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where the Cathedral sits, was undergoing gentrification pressures and becoming increasingly populated by young professionals and students, shifting the traditional character of the downtown core.

---

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Trump was in the midst of trade war escalation, having announced steel and aluminum tariffs in March and implementing them despite exemptions for Canada and Mexico; negotiations with China were intensifying. The Trump administration was actively implementing the "zero tolerance" immigration policy at the southern border, which would lead to family separations and became a national controversy reaching full public awareness in early June 2018. The James Comey book "A Higher Loyalty" had just been released (April 2018) and was generating ongoing political controversy about the Trump-FBI relationship. The NFL kneeling controversy from 2017 remained culturally present, with Trump continuing to attack players and the movement gaining further mainstream support.

---

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Trump administration's withdrawal from the Iran nuclear deal (JCPOA) had occurred on May 8, 2018, just 19 days before this sermon, with sanctions set to resume—a major geopolitical event affecting Middle Eastern tensions and US-European relations. The Winter Olympics aftermath in South Korea was fading, but North Korea remained in diplomatic flux following the Singapore summit announcement (scheduled for June 12, 2018), creating uncertainty about US-North Korean relations. The Israeli-Palestinian conflict was active, with heightened tensions and violence in Gaza following the opening of the US Embassy in Jerusalem on May 14, 2018.

---

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was experiencing profound polarization and institutional distrust, with the Mueller investigation into the 2016 election ongoing and dominating news cycles. The #MeToo movement, which had exploded in late 2017, was still reshaping conversations about power and accountability across institutions including the church. Culturally, Marvel's "Infinity War" had dominated the box office in May 2018 (released April 27), and the World Cup in Russia was being anticipated for June. The general zeitgeist was one of anxiety—economic uncertainty despite low unemployment, political chaos perceived through partisan lenses, and questions about America's role in the world amid trade wars and NATO tensions.
